# Phase 9.2.5 Governance Stability Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, or autonomous governance.

## Objective

Perform final integrated constitutional governance stability validation across all civilization governance doctrine.

## Scope

- Final governance stability doctrine.
- Civilization coherence validation.
- Integrated survivability review.
- Constitutional durability assessment.
- Anti-escalation continuity validation.
- Restraint stability review.
- Skepticism continuity validation.
- Archival integrity validation.
- Fail-closed civilization stability doctrine.

## Required Safeguards

- No runtime activation exists.
- No provider execution exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No deployment authority exists.
- No execution-capable infrastructure exists.
- No stability-review system creates runtime authority.
- No survivability-validation process bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No continuity-review layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No convergence-validation system creates infrastructure activation authority.
- No stability-governance framework weakens auditability.

## Step Improvement Notes

- Correct build order: follows civilization governance consolidation.
- Safe to include now: final non-runtime stability validation and checklists.
- Future upgrade: tribunal evidence admissibility, docket integrity, and record authenticity review.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, API integrations, background jobs, autonomous governance, or execution systems.
